title: Reactor high temperature, pressure and conversion response
content: Verify reactor cooling water system availability and cooling water valve XMV(10) position. Check reactor temperature XMEAS(9), reactor pressure XMEAS(7), cooling water outlet temperature XMEAS(21), and reactor feed conditions. Review recent changes to feed rates, catalyst activity, and recycle flow. Confirm automatic control loops are functioning correctly. Do not bypass safety interlocks or trip logic. If temperature or pressure continues to rise, reduce feed rates as per operating limits, notify the control room supervisor, and escalate to process engineering. Record all observations and corrective actions in the shift log.

title: Condenser performance degradation and cooling response
content: Monitor condenser outlet temperature, separator inlet conditions, and condenser duty indicators. Verify cooling water supply pressure, flow, and return temperature. Check cooling water valves for correct operation and inspect for fouling indications. Review condenser approach temperature trends and ensure downstream separator conditions remain stable. Investigate abnormal increases in vapor carryover or condenser outlet temperature. Coordinate with utilities personnel if cooling water availability is reduced. Escalate if condenser performance impacts separator operation, product recovery, or causes process instability.

title: Product separator level, pressure and phase separation response
content: Monitor separator pressure XMEAS(12), liquid level XMEAS(15), and separator temperature XMEAS(11). Verify inlet flow conditions from the reactor and condenser. Check outlet control valves and confirm level control loop performance. Inspect for excessive vapor carryover, liquid entrainment, or unstable interface levels. Ensure cooling water support systems are operating correctly. If pressure rises or liquid level approaches operating limits, notify operations supervision and initiate corrective actions according to operating procedures. Escalate abnormal pressure excursions immediately.

title: Stripper steam, separation efficiency and product quality check
content: Check stripper temperature indicators XMEAS(18) and XMEAS(19), steam valve position XMV(9), and product quality measurements XMEAS(37) through XMEAS(41). Verify steam supply pressure and control valve responsiveness. Review column temperature profile and assess separation efficiency. Investigate deviations in product composition, overhead losses, or bottom product quality. Confirm instrumentation is functioning correctly and no alarms are active. If quality specifications are not achieved, coordinate with process engineering and document corrective actions.

title: Recycle compressor abnormal work, surge and trip response
content: Monitor compressor work XMEAS(20), recycle flow XMEAS(5), recycle valve position XMV(5), suction pressure, and discharge pressure trends. Verify anti-surge control system functionality and confirm recycle valve response. Investigate abnormal vibration, temperature increases, or compressor efficiency loss. Review recent process disturbances that may affect compressor loading. Do not disable compressor protection systems. Escalate compressor trip alarms immediately and coordinate with maintenance and reliability teams. Record all actions and operating conditions during the event.

title: A feed system flow, pressure and quality verification
content: Verify A feed flow rate, feed pressure, and feed temperature are within operating limits. Confirm feed control valve operation and review flow controller performance. Check upstream storage and transfer systems for availability. Investigate flow fluctuations, pressure drops, or feed interruptions. Ensure feed quality specifications are met before entering the process. Coordinate with operations if feed composition changes are detected. Escalate prolonged feed disturbances that may impact reactor stability or production targets.

title: D feed system flow and supply reliability response
content: Monitor D feed flow, pressure, and temperature trends. Verify control valve performance and inspect for restrictions or abnormal pressure losses. Confirm feed availability from upstream sources and review inventory status. Investigate alarms related to low flow, low pressure, or instrumentation faults. Ensure feed composition remains within specification. Communicate any supply disruptions to the control room and process engineering teams. Document corrective actions and operational impacts.

title: E feed system operating condition verification
content: Check E feed flow rate, feed pressure, and feed temperature. Verify flow control loops are stable and valves respond correctly to controller outputs. Monitor feed quality indicators and identify any contamination risks. Review process demand and ensure feed delivery matches production requirements. Investigate abnormal fluctuations and coordinate with upstream operators if supply issues occur. Escalate sustained deviations affecting reactor performance or product quality.

title: A plus C feed blend system control and balance response
content: Verify combined feed flow, blend ratio targets, feed pressure, and feed temperature. Confirm both A and C feed sources are available and operating within specification. Review control valve positions and blend control strategy performance. Investigate ratio deviations, flow instability, or quality concerns. Ensure downstream reactor operating limits are not exceeded due to feed composition changes. Coordinate with process engineering for significant blend adjustments and document all corrective actions.

title: Reactor cooling water system availability and performance response
content: Monitor cooling water supply pressure, return pressure, supply temperature, return temperature, and total cooling water flow to the reactor. Verify cooling water pumps are operating correctly and no low-flow alarms are active. Inspect control valves, strainers, and heat transfer performance indicators. Investigate rising reactor temperatures that may indicate reduced cooling capacity. Coordinate with utilities personnel for cooling water quality or availability issues. Escalate immediately if cooling water loss threatens safe reactor operation.

title: Separator cooling water system monitoring and corrective action
content: Verify cooling water flow, supply temperature, return temperature, and pressure across separator cooling services. Check valve positions and cooling water distribution performance. Investigate separator temperature increases, poor condensation efficiency, or abnormal pressure trends that may be linked to cooling water limitations. Coordinate with utilities and operations teams to restore cooling performance. Escalate if separator operation, product recovery, or downstream equipment performance is affected by cooling water issues.
